_comment: Copy the block you need into your client's MCP settings.
Option A (stdio) - recommended for Cursor / Claude Desktop / most MCP clients:
The client launches server.py as a subprocess and injects MySQL parameters
via the 'env' section.  No separate server process needed.
Option B (sse) - standalone HTTP service; start with .venv python, e.g.:
Then point the client at the SSE URL.  MySQL parameters are configured
at server startup time via environment variables or a shell .env file.

mysql-readonly-stdio._comment: Option A: stdio. Use absolute paths. Windows command: ...\MySQL_MCP\.venv\Scripts\python.exe

mysql-readonly-sse._comment: Option B: SSE transport.  Server must be running before the client connects.
